when I run this it takes ages and it doesn't do anything -- can I get some insight into what its doing while its running?

The command makes one blocking, non-streaming call to a slow cloud reasoning model with a 600s timeout, printing nothing until it returns. Added a heartbeat spinner to stderr showing elapsed time during the call.
is it multistep? or does it literally take all that long in a single call? how many tokens do we send?

It's a single call sending roughly 1,700 tokens (catalog of guide titles/summaries, no bodies). The latency comes from the model's reasoning chain and cloud queueing, not payload size; suggested using a lighter model or enabling streaming if faster feedback is needed.
